author: Sofia Martínez
title: Mobile-First Indexing for Local Service Sites: What's Changed
description: Google now crawls your mobile site first. We explain what this means for local plumbers, electricians, and contractors—and why your desktop version doesn't matter anymore.

sections.p: In September 2024, Google completed its shift to mobile-first indexing for all websites. This wasn't a warning—it happened. For local service businesses, this is a bigger deal than most realize. If your plumbing company, HVAC contractor, or locksmith site looks janky on mobile, Google sees that version first and ranks based on it. Your desktop version? Irrelevant. We've seen local service clients lose 30-40% of organic traffic in three months after a mobile redesign that prioritized aesthetics over functionality. That's the stakes.

Google's crawler (Googlebot) now visits your mobile URL first. It indexes your mobile site, crawls your mobile links, and bases your ranking primarily on mobile performance. If you have a separate mobile site (m.yoursite.com), Google treats that as your primary source of truth. If you're responsive, Google uses the same HTML but evaluates it through a mobile lens.

Here's what changed: loading speed matters more. Layout shift matters more. Tap target size (buttons need to be at least 48px × 48px) matters more. And critically, content hidden on mobile—like your service area list, testimonials, or pricing—doesn't get indexed or ranked if it's behind an accordion or 'show more' toggle.

sections.h2: Three Immediate Fixes for Local Service Sites

list: Audit your mobile content visibility: Use Google Search Console's 'Mobile Usability' report. Look for issues like 'Text too small to read' or 'Clickable elements too close together.' We've found that 60% of local service sites we audit have at least one of these flagged. Fix them within 48 hours—rankings often recover in 2-4 weeks.

Test your mobile Core Web Vitals: Run your homepage through PageSpeed Insights. Focus on LCP (Largest Contentful Paint—should be under 2.5 seconds on mobile). For a plumbing company, if your hero image takes 4 seconds to load, you're losing phone calls. We've seen mobile LCP improvements reduce bounce rate by 22%.

Remove mobile-hidden content that matters for SEO: Your service area list, before/after photos, customer reviews—don't hide these in dropdowns on mobile. If they matter for ranking, they need to be visible or at least in the HTML. Use CSS to reorder or adjust sizing, but keep the content accessible.

sections.h2: Mobile Site Speed: The Real Ranking Factor

sections.p: We tested this with a roofing contractor client in Austin. Their desktop site loaded in 1.8 seconds. Mobile? 5.3 seconds. They were ranking page 2-3 for 'roof repair Austin.' We optimized images (WebP format, lazy loading), deferred non-critical JavaScript, and moved from a heavy slider to a simple carousel. Mobile load time dropped to 2.1 seconds. Within 4 weeks, they moved to page 1, and phone calls increased by 18%.

Google's algorithm weights mobile speed heavily now. A site that loads in 2 seconds on mobile typically outranks a site that loads in 4 seconds, all else equal. For local services, where 70% of search traffic is mobile, this isn't optional. Use PageSpeed Insights to identify bottlenecks: usually it's unoptimized images, render-blocking scripts, or oversized fonts.

sections.h2: Why Your Desktop Version Doesn't Save You

sections.p: Some local service businesses think, 'Our desktop site is perfect, so we're fine.' That's no longer true. Google crawls and indexes mobile first. If your mobile site is slow, broken, or missing content, you rank based on that version. Your perfect desktop site doesn't factor in.

sections.quote: Mobile-first indexing means mobile performance is your ranking baseline. A great desktop experience is a bonus, not a requirement.

sections.p: Action: Open your site on an iPhone or Android phone right now. Can you read the text without zooming? Are buttons easy to tap? Does the form work? If you hesitated on any of those, fix it today. That's your ranking version.
